headline: US airline stocks climb premarket as oil prices tumble on Iran ceasefire
summary: Investing.com -- U.S. airline stocks jumped in premarket trading on Wednesday as oil prices plunged following a conditional ceasefire agreement between the United States and Iran, de-escalating a conflict that has gripped energy markets for more than five weeks.

headline: Here Are the Stocks Rising the Most (and Some Losers)
summary: The market is rallying on hopes of a resumption of energy flows from the Middle East. Here are a few of the stocks moving the most as some of the big moves of recent weeks start to unwind. Going up: ✈️ Shares in Delta Air Lines (DAL), United Airlines (UAL) and Southwest Airlines (LUV), which have been walloped by higher fuel prices, are surging premarket.

headline: Delta Air Lines Flags $2 Billion Iran Fuel Hit. Why the Stock Is Flying After Earnings.
summary: Delta Air Lines stock was surging ahead of the open Wednesday as the carrier’s earnings and the two-week U.S., Iran cease-fire deal proved to be tailwinds for the shares.  Demand is so strong that Delta expects to post a $1 billion pretax profit in the current quarter despite a more than $2 billion increase in fuel costs.  The carrier is also cutting capacity to mitigate the impact of higher fuel prices.

The carrier is also cutting capacity to mitigate the impact of higher fuel prices.

headline: Delta Soars 13% Despite a Fuel Bill That Ballooned by $2 Billion
summary: Delta Air Lines (NYSE:DAL) shares are up 13% in early trading Wednesday morning, recovering sharply after yesterday’s close of $65.62. headline: Delta Air Lines Stock Surges After Earnings. How It’s Shrugging Off $2 Billion Fuel Hit.
summary: Delta Air Lines  stock was surging ahead of the open Wednesday as the carrier’s earnings and the two-week U.S., Iran cease-fire deal proved to be tailwinds for the shares.  Demand is so strong that Delta expects to post a $1 billion pretax profit in the current quarter despite a more than $2 billion increase in fuel costs.  The carrier is also cutting capacity to mitigate the impact of higher fuel prices, it said.

headline: Delta Stock Soars After Earnings. These Airlines Are Climbing Even Higher.
summary: Delta Air Lines  stock was surging 8% on Wednesday.  United Airlines  rose 13%,  Southwest Airlines  jumped 11%, and  Norwegian Cruise Line  was rising 12%.  Delta’s guidance may be a reason it’s lagging behind slightly—if an 8% rise can described in such terms.

headline: Oil Prices Bounce; Iran Says Hormuz Opening Hinges On Israel, But S&P 500 Rallies
summary: Crude oil prices plunged more than $20 a barrel from Tuesday's peak after President Donald Trump agreed to a two-week ceasefire and Iran agreed to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.  United Airlines, Carnival and fellow cruise and airline stocks still ranked among top S&P 500 winners in morning trade.  Gold, silver and copper prices all jumped, fueling gains for Newmont and Freeport-McMoRan, though they came well off their highs.

content.title: Oil Prices Bounce; Iran Says Hormuz Opening Hinges On Israel, But S&P 500 Rallies
content.summary: Crude oil prices plunged more than $20 a barrel from Tuesday's peak after President Donald Trump agreed to a two-week ceasefire and Iran agreed to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Gold, silver and copper prices all jumped, fueling gains for Newmont and Freeport-McMoRan, though they came well off their highs.
